title: verbascum × ambigens
intro: Verbascum × ambigens, also known as the biennial mullein, is a hybrid plant species within the Verbascum genus, resulting from the cross between Verbascum phoeniceum and Verbascum thapsus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, making it useful for soothing respiratory issues and wounds. The health benefits of Verbascum × ambigens include relief from congestion, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots can be used to prepare various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
